div.h1: TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION: CHROOT ESCAPE VS. PIVOT_ROOT ISOLATION
div.h2: Analysis of Mount Namespace Boundaries and VFS Traversal

g.text: Exploit State Machine: chroot(2) Jailbreak Sequence

div.p: The chroot system call only updates the task_struct->fs->root pointer. It does not modify the mount namespace. If a process holds a file descriptor referencing a path outside the new root, or navigates up using relative paths before the kernel can re-verify the root boundary, the process can re-gain access to the host filesystem.
pivot_root solves this by physically moving the mount point and allowing the host's old root to be unmounted entirely from the container's namespace.
